content.problemStatement:
Chef is busy preparing for the next lunchtime, so he has given you the following task.

You are given an array aaa consisting of nnn positive integers. The goodnessgoodnessgoodness of the array aaa is defined as the GCDGCDGCD of all the elements in the array.
Formally, goodness=GCD(a1,a2,‚Ä¶,an)goodness = GCD(a_1, a_2, \dots, a_n)goodness=GCD(a1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,an‚Äč).

You can perform the following type of operation:


Select any two elements of the array aia_iai‚Äč and aja_jaj‚Äč (i‚Č†ji \ne jiÓÄ†=j and 1‚Č§i,j‚Č§n 1 \leq i, j \leq n1‚Č§i,j‚Č§n) and replace both aia_iai‚Äč and aja_jaj‚Äč with ai‚čÖaja_i\cdot a_jai‚Äč‚čÖaj‚Äč.
You can perform this operation **at most once**. Find the **maximum goodness** of the array.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer nnn.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,ana_1, a_2, \dots, a_na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,an‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing a single integer representing the maximum goodness of the array.
constraints: The sum of nnn over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot10^52‚čÖ105
It is guaranteed that the maximum goodness will not exceed 101810^{18}1018
The sum of nnn over all cases does not exceed 200020002000
The sum of nnn over all cases does not exceed 10510^5105
